source.title: Demo reviewed polyuria vomiting case
source.citation: Local reviewed educational case file for Diagnostician demo content.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled and reviewed for demo validation.

case.title: Vomiting with polyuria and deep breathing
teaching_points: Polyuria, vomiting, dehydration, hyperglycemia, ketones, and an anion-gap acidosis form the diagnostic pattern.
Deep breathing can be compensation for metabolic acidosis.
Treatment decisions require attention to potassium as well as insulin and fluids.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient reports two days of vomiting, weakness, and increasing thirst.

provenance.quote: Two days vomiting, weakness, and increasing thirst.

facts.label: Urinary symptoms
facts.value: She has been urinating frequently and waking at night to drink water.

facts.label: Diabetes history
facts.value: She has type 1 diabetes and missed several insulin doses while ill.

provenance.quote: Type 1 diabetes; missed insulin doses during illness.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Heart rate is 128/min, blood pressure 96/58 mmHg, respiratory rate 30/min, temperature 37.6 C, oxygen saturation 99% on room air.

facts.label: General exam
facts.value: She appears dehydrated with dry mucous membranes and deep, labored respirations.

provenance.quote: Dry mucous membranes and deep labored respirations.

facts.label: Point-of-care glucose
facts.value: Capillary glucose reads high, and serum glucose is 612 mg/dL.

facts.label: Metabolic panel
facts.value: Sodium is 130 mmol/L, potassium 5.4 mmol/L, bicarbonate 8 mmol/L, and anion gap 28.

facts.label: Ketone testing
facts.value: Serum beta-hydroxybutyrate is markedly elevated and urine ketones are positive.

provenance.quote: Markedly elevated beta-hydroxybutyrate and positive urine ketones.

facts.label: Venous blood gas
facts.value: Venous pH is 7.12 with low bicarbonate.

facts.label: Chest radiograph
facts.value: Chest radiograph shows no focal infiltrate.

provenance.quote: No focal infiltrate on chest radiograph.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A hyperglycemic metabolic emergency should be considered before attributing vomiting to a simple gastrointestinal illness.

provenance.quote: Vomiting, dehydration, hyperglycemia, ketones, and acidosis support metabolic emergency.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Connect the deep breathing to the acid-base data rather than treating it as a primary lung problem.

provenance.quote: Deep breathing plus low bicarbonate points to metabolic acidosis compensation.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Diabetic ketoacidosis supported by missed insulin, hyperglycemia, ketones, anion-gap acidosis, and dehydration.
